functions: Oncogenic GTPase driving proliferative signaling when mutated (e.g., Q61L, Q61H, G12C-targetable)
Activates downstream ERK1/2 (MAPK) signaling promoting cell proliferation
Drives clonal expansion of initiated stem cells in tumor initiation
Mutant KRAS reprograms ER stress pathways (downregulates HRD1, induces ERO1L via HIF1α stabilization)
Reciprocal cross-interaction with SRSF1 splicing factor in pancreatic cancer
Promotes inflammation-driven pancreatic tumorigenesis
pathways: Integrated stress response (ISR) / PERK signaling (resistance context)

disease_associations.disease: Cutaneous (skin) squamous tumors / chemically induced skin carcinoma

pubmed.druggability_notes: Targetable by covalent inhibitor ARS-1620 (binds mutant KRAS; off-target ALDH1A3 via C314); KRAS Q61 mutations (Q61H, Q61L) define distinct NSCLC subgroups; structure-based KRAS-targeting peptides (Peptide-1) under investigation for colorectal cancer; acquired KRAS inhibitor (KRASi) resistance via ATF3-ASNS asparagine reprogramming, reversible by PERK inhibition.

functions: Signal transducer in the Ras-MAPK signaling pathway regulating cell proliferation and survival
Binds GDP/GTP and possesses intrinsic GTPase activity
Activates MAPK1/MAPK3 leading to phosphorylation and degradation of GJA1
Induces transcriptional silencing of tumor suppressor genes in a ZNF304-dependent manner
Promotes oncogenic events in colorectal cancer cells
Substrate for LZTR1-mediated ubiquitination via a BCR (BTB-CUL3-RBX1) E3 ligase complex
Positive regulation of cellular senescence
Cardiac and myoblast cell proliferation
uniprot.druggability_notes: KRAS is a GTP-binding hydrolase and proto-oncogene. Its GTPase activity and GDP/GTP nucleotide-binding pocket are central to function. Post-translational lipid modifications (palmitoylation, prenylation) drive membrane localization. The text does not explicitly describe small-molecule binding pockets or specific drug classes, but the nucleotide-binding site and prenylation/palmitoylation are mechanistically relevant for targeting.

functions: Binds GDP/GTP and possesses intrinsic GTPase activity
Regulates cell proliferation
Activates MAPK1/MAPK3 leading to phosphorylation and degradation of GJA1
Promotes oncogenic transcriptional silencing of tumor suppressor genes in colorectal cancer via ZNF304
Functions as a molecular switch in receptor tyrosine kinase signal transduction
Activates the RAF/MAP kinase cascade
pathways: RAS signaling downstream of NF1 loss-of-function variants (R-HSA-6802953)
opentargets.druggability_notes: Established proto-oncogene and major therapeutic target in oncology; the provided text does not detail specific binding pockets or drug classes, though RAS GTPase mutant signaling (e.g., R-HSA-9753512) and RAS GAP mutant pathways indicate mutation-driven actionability.
